It catches WHOLLY-disjoint vocab ('teleport to the moon and bake a cake' -> 0.00) but NOT partial content-word collisions: this scrape/email request scores 0.324 vs trace.ingest (>0.08 gate) on 'summary'/'page'/'ingest', so it still compiles. sentence: (adversarial-review finding — not a user sentence)
error: Template-time governance gate bypass (PR #77 adversarial review, P1 conf 0.80). governance_gate() substring-scans stage.args AS WRITTEN, but {"from":"upstream[.key]"} refs resolve to the upstream stage's OUTPUT at execution time (ix-pipeline::lower::resolve_from_refs). A stage like cache {operation:{from:'prep.op'}} serializes with no Article-3 trigger word, passes the gate, then the executor resolves operation->'delete' and runs it. Bounded exploitability (an upstream skill must EMIT the trigger string at runtime; a literal would trip that stage's own gate), but the gate is structurally template-only with NO post-resolution check. MITIGATED this PR: removed the 'unbypassable' overclaim; the gate now surfaces from-refs as unvetted_runtime_inputs in its verdict. DURABLE FIX = the `unify` increment: push the constitutional check into ix-pipeline::execute()/the lower compute closure so check_action sees the literal RESOLVED operation just before (desc.fn_ptr). Third-order dogfood finding, 2026-06-06 — the adversarial review found a structural gap in the gate the loop itself built.

sentence: (resolution of governance-resolved-args)
error: CLOSED 2026-06-07 (the `unify` PR). The durable fix shipped as a trait seam, NOT the originally-planned hard ix-governance dep on ix-pipeline: new `ix_pipeline::gate::StageGate` trait (+ AllowAll default) + `lower_with_gate(spec, gate)` consults the gate on each stage's RESOLVED args inside the compute closure, after resolve_from_refs and before (desc.fn_ptr). ix-skill supplies `ConstitutionGate` (Demerzel-backed); `ix pipeline run` + `compile --run` both lower WITH it. SCOPE: closed for the canonical PipelineSpec path only — the legacy System A MCP `ix_pipeline_run` ({steps} substitution in ix-agent/src/tools.rs) does NOT lower with a gate and stays ungated until {steps} retirement (D2); that's the open security follow-up (Codex #78 P2). ix-pipeline stays governance-agnostic (no new dep on the foundational crate — better blast radius than planned). Proven compositionally (no registry skill emits a trigger word without a literal an earlier gate catches = the bound): (A) resolved_gate_sees_post_resolution_args_not_the_template, (B) constitution_gate_rejects_resolved_destructive_op, (C) rejecting_gate_aborts_stage_before_skill_runs. The dogfood loop closed a gap it surfaced.

sentence: --help
(measurement — embeddings front baseline)
error: Built a 184-probe validated labeled corpus (state/thinking-machine/coverage-probes.jsonl: 104 in-domain, 56 near-miss-OOD, 24 far-OOD; fan-out gen + adversarial re-label vs full 52-skill catalog, 1 dropped). Measured the live lexical TF-IDF coverage_score @ threshold 0.08 (test coverage_baseline_tfidf_over_probe_corpus): in-domain recall 0.990 but overall OOD TNR only 0.163 (far_ood 0.458, near_miss 0.036) — confabulates ~84% of out-of-domain. SURPRISE: the pre-gate is far weaker than the 'good-on-far-OOD, weak-on-near-miss' narrative; it's weak across the board, and the fail-OPEN LLM relevance tier silently carries refusal. This is the baseline the embeddings gate must beat (raise TNR, hold recall >= ~0.97). Fourth dogfood finding, 2026-06-07 — instrument-before-building (per deep-research open question) revealed the real target. Per reference_embedding_coverage_gate_research.
